Note: The census covered agricultural holdings that met the following thresholds: 25 m2 of arable land; one head of cattle; five goats/sheep/pigs; or 50 chicken/ducks/turkeys. The census covered smallholders in rural areas only and all large-scale farms. Urban and peri-urban areas were not covered by the census.

The census definition of a farm is any place from which $1,000 or more of agricultural products were produced and sold, or normally would have been sold, during the census year.
